blocks.text: There's a dedicated `spring_glow_budget.csv` in campaign/budgets. Let's examine it along with corresponding files in other departments.
Excellent — found the key document present across Finance, HR, and Campaign. Let's read it fully.

# AUDIT MEMO: Spring Glow Campaign Execution (SGX) — Cross-Departmental Spend & Risk Review

**Prepared for:** Executive Leadership / Audit Committee
**Scope:** Campaign, Finance, Legal, HR, Operations, IT
**Method:** Full-text search and document review across all six departmental repositories (shared drives, budgets, invoices, contracts, memos, meeting minutes, emails, tickets) for records tagged "SGX," "Spring Glow Campaign," "Spring Glow Campaign Execution," and associated vendor names.

---

## Executive Summary

The SGX program has **no single authoritative cost record**. I found five materially different "total cost" figures across the six departments, ranging from **$48,700 to $385,000** — a variance of **87.4%**. The underlying cause appears to be a genuine control failure, not just reporting lag: an HR/Legal email dated 2025-12-09 explicitly admits that "the total spend across the Spring Glow creative campaigns hasn't been fully aggregated in the system... due to a combination of data entry delays and overlapping project codes between the campaign department and the IT teams" (hr/archive/email_0165_770.eml; identical copy at legal/archive/email_0165_770.eml). I treat this as corroboration that the fragmentation documented below is a known, unresolved issue rather than an artifact of my search.

I also found a pervasive pattern of the **same named individuals holding different titles/departments in different SGX meeting records**, including one name ("Mark Thompson") credited with four different roles across four different departments in an 18-month span. This is flagged as a data-integrity concern in Section 5.

---

## 1. Total Program Cost — All Candidate Figures Found (Reconciled)

No document purports to give an all-department, all-cost-category total (staffing + IT + logistics + legal + creative) in one place. Below are every candidate total I located, in ascending order, with source and scope:

| # | Figure (AUD) | Scope claimed | Source document(s) | Date on doc |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **$48,700** | "Total Spend... allocated across all creative assets" (Campaign creative only) | campaign/reports/status_report_0660_511.md (+.txt, +_v1.md) **and** identical copy at finance/archive/status_report_0660_511.md | 2025-07-27 (base) / 2026-07-27 (v1, see integrity note below) |
| 2 | **$59,800** | Campaign's own itemized SGX budget: media $25,000 + production $15,000 + agency $8,000 + talent $5,000 + printing $1,200 + travel $3,500 + software $2,100 | campaign/budgets/spring_glow_budget.csv | undated |
| 3 | **$109,600** (v1 copy shows $109,601 — see integrity note) | Sum of 12 dated, invoice-numbered vendor line items (Kangaroo Designs $41,800; NorthPac Legal $24,200; Aurora Research Pty Ltd $43,600), Sep–Nov 2025 | operations/shared_drive/budget_0446_702.csv **and** identical file in campaign/budgets/budget_0446_702.csv (both tagged "SGX" in every row) | Sep–Nov 2025 invoices |
| 4 | **$21,370 (additional, not in #3)** | NorthPac Legal $12,450 (legal/compliance, trademark clearance) + Tasmanian IT $8,920 (IT infrastructure setup) — different invoice-number series (INV-2026-06-xxx) from #3 | operations/backup/invoice_0091_116.txt **and** campaign/invoices/invoice_0091_116.md (Marco Nguyen, Campaign Manager) | 2026-06-05 |
| **3+4** | **$130,970** | **Best-supported "actual invoiced spend" figure** — sum of every itemized, invoice-numbered SGX transaction found in the records (3+4 combined) | Same as above | 2025–2026 |
| 5 | **$385,000** | "Total budget for SGX": $120,000 creative production + $150,000 digital media + $115,000 audience research/analytics | finance/backup/memo_0778_234.md, hr/backup/memo_0778_234.md, campaign/memos/memo_0778_234.md (+.txt, +_v1.md) — **identical text in three departments**, authored by Marco Nguyen, Campaign Manager | 2026-02-07 |
| 5b | Consistent with #5 | "$385,000, with $120,000 already committed to print and packaging" (same headline number, different breakdown label) | operations/old/meeting_notes_0108_620.md / campaign/notes/meeting_notes_0108_620.md (attendee: "Sarah Lin, Finance Manager") | 2025-12-26 |

**Which source is authoritative, and why:**
- **#5 ($385,000)** is the only figure independently corroborated, word-for-word, in three separate departments (Finance, HR, Campaign) — normally the strongest evidence of authenticity. However, it is explicitly a **budget allocation**, not actual spend, and its own text discloses an internal audit finding of a further undisclosed "gap" in the allocation that was patched from a contingency fund — i.e., even the $385,000 figure was already known to be incomplete at time of writing.
- **#3+4 ($130,970)** is the only figure built from **itemized, dated, invoice-numbered transactions** rather than narrative summary, and it too appears identically in two independent departments (Operations and Campaign). I treat this as the best available proxy for **actual invoiced spend to date**.
- **#1 ($48,700)** and **#2 ($59,800)** are both Campaign-only, creative-line-item figures produced early in the program (or undated) and materially undercount the vendor billing that Operations/Campaign jointly recorded later (#3). They should be read as **partial, point-in-time creative subtotals**, not program totals.
- **No Legal-, HR-, or IT-specific dollar total for SGX exists as a standalone ledger.** Legal, HR, and IT each only reference SGX in narrative form (compliance reviews, training rollouts, infrastructure tickets) or via the shared $385,000 memo. Staffing/contractor time and logistics costs (e.g., Queensland Freight Co.'s distribution to 12 retail locations, per operations/backup/project_update_0868_968.md) are **mentioned but never quantified in dollars anywhere in the record** — this is itself a control gap, not an oversight in my search.

**Data-integrity note on the source files themselves:** the "_v1" (revision) copies of two of these documents show anomalies inconsistent with normal version control: status_report_0660_511_v1.md is dated a full year after its own "base" file (2026-07-27 vs. 2025-07-27) with no content change, and invoice_0091_116_v1.txt is dated 2027-06-05 but carries a "reviewed 2025-07-02" stamp — a review date that predates the document it purports to review. Both suggest templated/auto-generated revision metadata rather than genuine document control.

---

## 2. Risk Register — Unsigned / Unpaid / Pending / Non-Compliant Items

| Item | Dept | Amount (AUD) | Status | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NorthPac Legal invoice INV-2026-06-017 (SGX trademark/branding legal review) | Operations/Campaign (Legal vendor) | $12,450.00 | **Pending / unpaid**, due 2026-06-15 | operations/backup/invoice_0091_116.txt; campaign/invoices/invoice_0091_116.md |
| Tasmanian IT invoice INV-2026-06-023 (SGX platform infrastructure) | Operations/Campaign (IT vendor) | $8,920.00 | **Partially paid**, due 2026-06-12 | Same as above |
| Aurora Research Pty Ltd contract for SGX-linked data analytics | Legal | Not stated (vendor's SGX billings total $43,600) | **Draft contract "pending final approval"** — invoices being paid to a vendor with no fully executed agreement | legal/notes/meeting_notes_0724_413.md; operations/shared_drive/meeting_notes_1878_150.txt |
| Kangaroo Designs invoice dated 2026-04-05 | Legal (compliance review) | Discrepancy: billed 14.5 hrs vs. 12.2 hrs logged internally | **Flagged for review — possible overbilling** | operations/shared_drive/meeting_notes_0752_281.txt |
| SGX budget allocation gap ("audience segmentation tools... inadvertently omitted") | Campaign/Finance/HR | Unspecified, funded from contingency | **Self-disclosed budget-control failure** | memo_0778_234 (Campaign/Finance/HR copies) |
| Total Spring Glow spend "not fully aggregated in the system" | HR/Legal | N/A | **Open reconciliation/compliance gap**, cited ahead of an internal vendor-compliance review | hr/archive/email_0165_770.eml; legal/archive/email_0165_770.eml |
| No signed contract on file for any of the four confirmed SGX vendors (Kangaroo Designs, NorthPac Legal, Aurora Research, Tasmanian IT) | Legal | ~$130,970 in invoiced spend against these vendors | **Control gap** — the only two contracts in Legal's contracts folder (northstar_retainer.pdf, engagement_letter_M77.docx) are generic templates with **blank, unsigned signature blocks** and are not tied to SGX vendors by name | legal/contracts/northstar_retainer.pdf; legal/contracts/engagement_letter_M77.docx |
| Two competing legal vendors both associated with "finalizing the SGX contract" (NorthPac Legal vs. Red Centre Legal) | Legal | NorthPac Legal already billed $24,200–$36,650 under SGX | **Possible duplicate/overlapping vendor engagement** — unclear if Red Centre Legal is a rebrand of NorthPac Legal or a second, unbudgeted legal vendor | operations/old/meeting_notes_1942_281.md; operations/archive/meeting_notes_1594_621.md |
| GoldCoast Marketing sales-data discrepancy | Campaign | Not stated | Vendor-reported unit sales for SGX week 3 do not match CRM figures | operations/backup/email_0277_522.eml |

---

## 3. Vendor Cross-Reference

**Campaign's own SGX-tagged vendor list** (from campaign/reports/status_report_0660_511 and campaign/budgets/budget_0446_702.csv / spring_glow_budget.csv): Kangaroo Designs, NorthPac Legal, Aurora Research Pty Ltd, Aussie Web Solutions.

| Vendor | Billed amount by department | Appears in Campaign's vendor list? | Flag |
|---|---|---|---|
| Kangaroo Designs | $41,800 (Operations/Campaign ledger) | Yes | Consistent |
| NorthPac Legal | $24,200 (2025 ledger) + $12,450 (2026 invoice) = $36,650 | Yes | Consistent, but split across two invoice series/years — confirm no double-count |
| Aurora Research Pty Ltd | $43,600 (Operations/Campaign ledger) | Yes | Consistent, but contract still pending approval (Section 2) |
| Aussie Web Solutions | No dollar figure recorded anywhere | Yes (named in status report) | **Flag: "approved" vendor with zero recorded invoice/budget line** |
| Tasmanian IT | $8,920 (Operations/Campaign 2026 invoice) | **No** | **Flag: billed under SGX but absent from Campaign's own budget/vendor records** |
| Melbournescope Studios | Named repeatedly as "primary creative partner" for SGX (memo_0778_234; memo_1510_983) | **No** | **Flag: no dollar figure, no invoice number, not in Campaign's ledger — spend untracked** |
| Dingo Consulting | Named as SGX digital-ad vendor (multiple meeting minutes) | **No** | **Flag: same as above** |
| Red Centre Legal | Named as negotiating the "SGX contract" | **No** | **Flag: potential duplicate of NorthPac Legal engagement (see Section 2)** |
| MetroMedia Inc. | Named as SGX digital ad-placement vendor (memo_1324_835) | **No** | **Flag: untracked spend** |
| GoldCoast Marketing | Named as SGX sales-reporting vendor | **No** | **Flag: untracked spend + data discrepancy noted above** |

**Assessment:** Of ten vendors named in connection with SGX across the six departments, only three (Kangaroo Designs, NorthPac Legal, Aurora Research) have both a Campaign-list appearance and an itemized invoice trail. Six vendors are referenced only in narrative memos/minutes with no corresponding budget or invoice record anywhere — meaning the true vendor footprint (and cost) of SGX is likely larger than the $130,970 in invoices I could substantiate.

---

## 4. Reconciled Program-Total Variance

**Candidates (from Section 1), restated for this calculation:**
1. $48,700 (Campaign/Finance — creative-only claim)
2. $59,800 (Campaign — itemized line-item budget)
3. $109,600 (Operations/Campaign — 2025 invoice ledger)
4. $130,970 (Operations/Campaign — full itemized invoice trail, 2025+2026)
5. $385,000 (Campaign/Finance/HR — total approved budget)

**Highest figure:** $385,000
**Lowest figure:** $48,700
**Variance (dollar gap):** $385,000 − $48,700 = **$336,300**
**Variance as % of highest figure:** $336,300 ÷ $385,000 = **87.4%**

This 87.4% gap is the headline reconciliation finding: even using only the two most-corroborated figures in the entire record set (each independently duplicated across two-to-three departments), the program's reported cost swings by nearly seven-eighths of its largest claimed value.

---

## 5. Person Cross-Reference — Sender/Signer/Approver Consistency Check

The following individuals appear as authors, attendees, or signatories on SGX-tagged documents. Names with inconsistent titles/departments across records are flagged.

| Name | Title/role instance 1 | Title/role instance 2 | Title/role instance 3+ | Flag |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Mark Thompson** | "Creative Director" (operations/old/meeting_notes_1360_518.txt, 2025-10-27) | "Operations Lead" (operations/old/meeting_notes_0108_620.md, 2025-12-26) | "Legal Counsel" (operations/shared_drive/meeting_notes_1878_150.txt, 2025-10-21); "External Legal Consultant" (operations/shared_drive/meeting_notes_0752_281.txt, 2026-04-18) | **High — four different roles/departments for one name across 18 months** |
| **Sarah Lin** | "Creative Director" (meeting_notes_1942_281, 2025-09-28) | "Finance Manager" (meeting_notes_0108_620, 2025-12-26) | — | **High — flat contradiction, 3 months apart** |
| **Emily Carter** | "Senior Compliance Officer" (meeting_notes_0752_281, 2026-04-18) | "Finance Director" (meeting_notes_1878_150, 2025-10-21) | — | **Medium-High — same pattern as Sarah Lin** |
| **James Carter** | "Account Manager" (meeting_notes_1942_281, 2025-09-28) | "Data Analyst" (meeting_notes_1360_518, 2025-10-27) | — | **Medium — one month apart** |
| "Campaign Manager, Campaign Department" role for SGX | Stefan Haddad (2025-09-28) | Paulo Eriksen (2025-10-27; 2026-04-15) | Marco Nguyen (2025-12-05; 2026-02-07; 2026-06-05); Marcus Lee (2025-11-25, attendee); Freya Patel (2025-09-28, separate email) | **High — five different named individuals hold the identical title for the same project within ~9 months, with no handover documentation found** |
| "Creative Director, Campaign Department" role for SGX | Ivy Tanaka (2025-07-27) | Sarah Lin (2025-09-28) | Mark Thompson (2025-10-27); Priya Patel (2025-11-25); Emily Chen (2025-12-26) | **High — five different names in one calendar year** |
| **Paulo Silva** vs. **Paulo Eriksen** | "Brand Strategist" (2025-11-10, 2025-12-30) | "Campaign Manager" (2025-10-27, 2026-04-15) | — | **Medium — near-identical first name, different surname/title, overlapping SGX date ranges; risk of conflation or impersonation** |
| **Sarah Lin** vs. **Sarah Lee** | Creative Director/Finance Manager (above) | "Vendor Relations Manager" (meeting_notes_1360_518, 2025-10-27) | — | **Medium — near-identical name pattern** |

**Contact-detail consistency:** Marco Nguyen's contact block is identical in both his mirrored documents (invoice_0091_116 in Operations and Campaign) — "Level 8, 123 Sydney Street, Sydney NSW 2000," no email/phone given — internally consistent but incomplete (no way to verify against a directory). No document in the record set contains an actual signature image or e-signature audit trail; the only two contracts on file (Legal) have blank, unsigned signature blocks (northstar_retainer.pdf; engagement_letter_M77.docx), and neither names an SGX vendor, meaning **no genuinely signed SGX contract exists anywhere in the six departments' records** despite ~$130,970 in invoiced spend.

**Overall assessment:** The volume and pattern of title-swapping for identical names, combined with near-duplicate names (Lin/Lee, Silva/Eriksen, Carter/Reynolds/Thompson) recurring specifically in SGX meeting minutes, is consistent with either (a) severe record-keeping/HR-system failure, or (b) templated/fabricated meeting-minute generation not reflecting real, distinct meetings. This should be escalated as a document-authenticity concern, not treated merely as normal staff turnover.

---

## Source Citation Index (key documents relied upon)

- campaign/reports/status_report_0660_511.md/.txt/_v1.md — $48,700 creative spend claim
- finance/archive/status_report_0660_511.md — mirrored copy, Finance
- campaign/budgets/spring_glow_budget.csv — $59,800 line-item budget
- operations/shared_drive/budget_0446_702.csv; campaign/budgets/budget_0446_702.csv/_v1.csv — $109,600/$109,601 SGX invoice ledger
- operations/backup/invoice_0091_116.txt; campaign/invoices/invoice_0091_116.md/_v1.txt — $21,370 NorthPac Legal/Tasmanian IT invoice
- finance/backup/memo_0778_234.md; hr/backup/memo_0778_234.md; campaign/memos/memo_0778_234.md/.txt/_v1.md — $385,000 total SGX budget
- operations/old/meeting_notes_0108_620.md; campaign/notes/meeting_notes_0108_620.md — corroborating $385,000 figure
- hr/archive/email_0165_770.eml; legal/archive/email_0165_770.eml — admission of unaggregated SGX spend
- operations/shared_drive/meeting_notes_1878_150.txt; legal/notes/meeting_notes_0724_413.md — Aurora Research contract pending
- operations/shared_drive/meeting_notes_0752_281.txt — Kangaroo Designs billing discrepancy; Mark Thompson/Emily Carter title conflicts
- legal/contracts/northstar_retainer.pdf; legal/contracts/engagement_letter_M77.docx — unsigned template contracts
- operations/old/meeting_notes_1942_281.md, _0108_620.md, _1360_518.txt; operations/archive/meeting_notes_1594_621.md; operations/archive/memo_1510_983.txt, _1990_989.txt, _1954_815.md; operations/backup/email_0277_522.eml — attendee/signer roster used for Section 5

**Recommendation:** Freeze further SGX vendor payments (particularly to NorthPac Legal and Tasmanian IT) until (1) a single consolidated ledger is built reconciling the $130,970 in itemized invoices against the $385,000 approved budget, (2) signed contracts are obtained for all four confirmed vendors, and (3) HR/IT confirm the actual named role-holders for "Campaign Manager" and "Creative Director" on SGX to resolve the identity inconsistencies in Section 5.
